data.title: MightyCall Named to Inc.'s 2026 List of the Fastest-Growing Private Companies in the Pacific
data.description: On April 2, 2026, in San Francisco, MightyCall (CallCurrent Inc.) secured the 19th spot on the prestigious Inc. Regionals: Pacific list. This list showcases the fastest-growing companies in the region, with companies boasting a median growth rate of 94%. The ranking is part of Inc.'s sixth annual compilation, highlighting the remarkable growth and success of businesses in the Pacific region.

data.title: Acuity, Lindsay And 3 Stocks To Watch Heading Into Thursday
data.description: Today, U.S. stock futures are down, leading investors to turn their focus on certain stocks. Acuity Inc (NYSE: AYI) is expected to announce quarterly earnings of $4.06 per share with revenue hitting $1.09 million before the market opens. Pre-market trading saw Acuity's shares rise by 1% to $289.85. Meanwhile, Visionary Holdings (NASDAQ: GV) saw a significant surge of over 42% in pre-market trading following an announcement of a US$20 million investment cooperation deal with Qingdao Xihai Rongke on Tuesday. For the full story, visit Benzinga.com.

data.title: Beyond Meat Stock Trending—Edges Lower In Pre-Market Amid Earnings Miss, Weak Q1 Guidance
data.description: Beyond Meat Inc. (NASDAQ: BYND) stocks are in focus as they experienced a 0.85% decrease in pre-market trading on Thursday, reaching $0.6150, following a fourth-quarter earnings report that fell short of expectations by 163.64%. This marks the fifth consecutive time the company has failed to meet its EPS estimates. Additionally, their first-quarter guidance was significantly lower than anticipated. The plant-based protein producer recorded an adjusted loss of 29 cents per share, missing the projected loss of 11 cents. Revenue for Beyond Meat stood at $61.59 million, below the estimated $62 million, marking a 19.7% drop compared to the previous year. The full article is available on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Trump Administration Prepares 100% Tariffs On Drugmakers Without Pricing Deals: Report
data.description: As per reports, the Trump administration is set to reveal 100% tariffs on pharmaceutical companies lacking pricing agreements with the White House. The announcement may happen soon, potentially on Thursday, sources informed Bloomberg. These tariffs are a result of a Section 232 probe under the Trade Expansion Act, citing national security reasons. Companies without deals or ongoing talks could face the full tariff. However, exemptions are possible for specific medicines and disease categories. The White House has not yet commented on the matter. Many major drug firms like Pfizer Inc., Eli Lilly and Co., AstraZeneca PLC, and Novo Nordisk have already secured deals to circumvent the tariffs. For more details, the complete article can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Oasis Responds to Kao Board’s Opposition: An Open Letter to Fellow Kao Shareholders (4452 JP)
data.description: In Hong Kong, an open letter to shareholders has been issued regarding Kao Corporation's upcoming Extraordinary General Meeting of Shareholders set for April 30, 2026. The letter discusses critical matters pertaining to governance at Kao Corporation, urging direct communication due to the significance of the issues at hand. The company's board has recently responded to a proposal, prompting a detailed response outlining governance concerns and supply chain risks. This communication aims to address these issues in preparation for the EGM.

data.description: A recent report indicates that the global population has reached 7.8 billion people, with the majority residing in Asia. The data also shows that the global economy grew by 3.5% last year, reaching a total of $87.5 trillion. The report highlights an increase in global trade, which rose by 2.3% compared to the previous year. Additionally, renewable energy sources saw a significant growth of 7.4% in 2020, accounting for 29.2% of the world's total energy consumption. The report suggests that these trends are likely to continue in the upcoming years.
